title: Sendblue Eve Channel Tasks
principles: Keep Sendblue contracts and services app-owned in apps/agent until a stable cross-channel abstraction is proven.
Authenticate sb-signing-secret in constant time before decoding or trusting any body field; missing config fails closed.
Use Effect Schema, tagged errors, Context services, Layers, Config, Redacted, HttpClient, and explicit live/memory persistence boundaries.
Use atomic durable claims for inbound message_handle and outbound Eve event coordinates; process-local replay state is forbidden.
Map only explicitly configured E.164 senders to stable Eve principals and opaque continuation tokens.
One shared Sendblue account and line may have exactly one active receive webhook outside an explicit maintenance cutover; Production owns that webhook.
Per-environment webhook secrets and replay stores are security and durability boundaries, not provider routing or cross-environment deduplication.
Helper sprawl is forbidden: retain only abstractions with multiple concrete call sites or ownership of a provider, security, atomic persistence, serialization, resource, or policy boundary.

This SPEC adds no React or visible operator route. This completed record retains the former leaf-owned state/command wording as historical evidence, not current policy; any frontend requires a separate SPEC following primitive -> composite -> layout -> route, route/feature-owned data and workflow execution, and presentation-leaf rendering/accessibility/local UI and state display.
Do not log or persist credentials, message text, full phone numbers, protected webhook URLs, or raw provider bodies in evidence.
globalVerification: Effect language-service diagnostics are reviewed for every changed TypeScript file with no unresolved actionable hints
No React/component/visible route files change; otherwise stop for a separate frontend SPEC and Browser verification
No broad lint suppressions, structural casts, generic helper modules, pass-through services, or dead exports
Direct HTTP proof for webhook auth/status behavior
Sanitized Vercel Preview and Production, Eve session, Sendblue provider, storage, runtime-log, Agent Run, proxy-correlation, Executor-policy, and leak-scan evidence
Provider readback proves exactly one active receive webhook on the shared account/line and its parsed host is the stable Production host
A bounded handset proof records one Production ingress/dispatch/model/outbound and zero Preview ingress/run/model/tool/outbound side effects

implementationImprovementAuditCounter.description: Before accepting each task, record separate parent audits for ownership/call graph, Effect implementation quality and helper admission, and verification/evidence coverage. Add passes while any authentication, identity, replay, delivery, PII, Vercel proof, or attempted frontend-composition finding remains.

tasks.title: Implement app-owned Sendblue contracts, config, and HTTP client
tasks.description: Create the canonical Effect Schema contracts, redacted Effect Config, tagged errors, and named SendblueClient service with live HTTP and deterministic memory/test Layers.
implementationNotes: Keep the boundary under Eve's supported import-only apps/agent/agent/lib/sendblue directory; do not create a workspace package, an unsupported direct agent/sendblue directory, or changes in @bundjil/core/@bundjil/eve-effect.
Define branded E.164, message-handle, principal, conversation, send input/success, webhook payload, claim, and sanitized result schemas and derive all TypeScript types from them.

Load API key/secret, webhook secret, line, identity mapping, routing key, service allowlist, API URL, and replay-store config through a distinct sendblue/config.ts using Effect Config, Config.redacted, the runtime environment provider, and deterministic ConfigProvider fixtures in tests.

Implement SendblueClient.sendMessage as a named Effect operation over Effect HttpClient or a narrowly wrapped SDK. Encode/decode JSON with Effect Schema and classify 2xx, 4xx, 401, 429, malformed, network, timeout, and uncertain outcomes.
Do not add typing, status, contacts, groups, media, or webhook-admin runtime operations unless required by the preview provisioning task and kept at the CLI edge.
Add only modules/services with a provider, config, serialization, security, or reusable policy owner; inline one-off transforms.
mandatoryVerification: Flat meaningful Effect.gen/Effect.fn programs for config load and sendMessage; typed errors handled in .pipe(...) with catchTag, catchTags, or mapError.

Canonical app-owned Effect Schema types; no unsafe casts, DTO mirrors, manual object readers/mappers, JSON.stringify/JSON.parse, direct process.env, raw fetch in the channel boundary, or trivial wrappers/helpers.
Config tests prove required fields, E.164/identity-map decoding, redaction, missing-secret fail-closed behavior, test-only base URL override, and no value leakage.
Client tests prove headers and schema-encoded body without secret/body snapshots, response decoding, provider error classes, timeout/uncertain classification, and no automatic uncertain retry.
Agent check-types/test/build and bun run verification pass.
completionCriteria: SendblueClient has explicit live and memory/test Layers and one named sendMessage operation.
All provider/config boundaries are canonical, redacted, and app-owned.
All three audit passes are recorded.

evidence: Ownership/call graph: app-owned contracts, config, HTTP client, and memory Layer live under Eve's supported agent/lib/sendblue import-only boundary; no shared package or provider state changed.

Implementation quality: canonical Schema-derived contracts, safe tagged errors without raw causes, flat named sendMessage flow, Config/Context/Layer/HttpClient/Match primitives, one provider decode, and no unsafe casts/manual JSON/helper sprawl; initial Config/service/error findings were corrected by the same Terra Medium worker.

Verification coverage: seven focused tests prove redacted fail-closed Config, E.164 and allowlist decoding, test-only URL override, exact synthetic headers/body, success/provider rejection/status/schema/transport/timeout behavior, memory Layer determinism, and leak-safe errors; agent typecheck/test/build, root verification, and diff checks pass.

tasks.title: Implement webhook verification, sender identity, routing, and replay services
tasks.description: Add fail-closed constant-time Sendblue shared-secret verification, allowlisted sender mapping, opaque conversation routing, and atomic inbound/outbound replay claims with memory and Upstash live Layers.
implementationNotes: SendblueWebhookVerifier receives Redacted config and verifies sb-signing-secret in constant time before any body decode. Length mismatch and absent configured secret are authentication failures.
SendblueIdentityDirectory maps normalized E.164 numbers from redacted config to stable non-phone principal ids. Unknown senders do not create a session.
SendblueSessionRouter uses a keyed digest of sender plus configured line to derive an opaque deterministic continuation token; raw phone numbers never become principal ids or channel metadata.

SendblueReplayStore exposes atomic claim/complete/retryable/uncertain operations. The Upstash Layer uses a single NX+TTL provider operation for claims rather than KeyValueStore get-then-set/modify defaults.
Use inbound message_handle-derived claim keys and outbound sessionId/turnId/stepIndex/sequence claim keys. Store opaque ids/status/timestamps only.
On storage failure fail closed with a typed error; do not dispatch or send without a durable claim.
mandatoryVerification: Flat named Effect programs with expected tagged errors handled in .pipe(...) and canonical Schema-derived contracts.
No unsafe casts, DTO mirrors, manual readers/mappers, raw JSON, process-local Map/Set replay state, timing-variable equality, direct process.env, or helper sprawl.
Verifier tests cover missing/malformed/mismatch/match and prove the body decoder is not invoked before successful auth.
Identity/routing tests cover E.164 normalization, mapped/unknown senders, line separation, stable opaque keys, and no phone number in principal/metadata.
Concurrency tests race identical/different inbound and outbound claims, prove exactly one winner, exercise lease expiry/retryable/complete/uncertain transitions, and model live atomic semantics.
Upstash adapter tests prove NX+TTL command shape and sanitized errors without using real credentials.
Agent checks and bun run verification pass.
completionCriteria: Authentication, identity, routing, and durable replay policies are independent named Effect services with live/memory Layers.
No request can reach body-derived identity or Eve dispatch before provider auth and atomic claim.
All three audit passes are recorded.

evidence: Ownership/call graph: Eve import-only app code owns independent verifier, identity, routing, replay, and secure claim-id services; live paths use Config and Upstash while tests inject Config, deterministic ids, synchronized memory state, and provider clients.

Implementation quality: platform timingSafeEqual authenticates before decode; HMAC/Encoding produces opaque keys; replay results are discriminated Schemas; SynchronizedRef plus HashMap models atomic memory behavior; Upstash claims use one SET NX EX and owner-fenced Lua transitions; review corrected raw JS comparison, manual hex, invalid optional-claim states, global UUIDs, stale-transition success, and inconsistent retryable semantics.

Verification coverage: ten focused security/replay tests cover missing, length-mismatched, equal-length-mismatched, and matching secrets; decode ordering; sender policy; line-separated opaque routing; concurrent identical inbound/outbound claims and independent keys; lease/complete/uncertain/retryable transitions; owner fencing; Upstash SET/eval arguments; and leak-safe provider failures. Frozen install, agent checks/build, root verification, and diff checks pass.

tasks.title: Implement the thin Eve route and outbound event adapter
tasks.description: Compose the Sendblue Effect services behind a custom Eve channel route, route accepted direct text into deterministic sessions, and deliver completed assistant text with outbound replay protection.
implementationNotes: Create apps/agent/agent/channels/sendblue.ts with defineChannel and POST /eve/v1/sendblue/webhook. It may run Effects at the framework edge but must contain no provider, parsing, identity, storage, or delivery policy.

Create SendblueChannel.handleInbound as the linear auth -> decode -> classify -> line/loop check -> identity -> route -> claim -> Eve dispatch program. Decode the request body once through Schema.fromJsonString or the canonical codec.
Acknowledge authenticated ignored and duplicate events without dispatch. Return 400 for authenticated malformed input, 401 for auth failure, and 503 when no durable claim can be made.
Support direct text-only iMessage receive events. Ignore outbound/status/typing/group/media-only/empty/unsupported-service events with sanitized outcomes.
Build Eve auth only after identity mapping and call send through waitUntil after a successful inbound claim. Durable channel state contains only fields required for reply routing.

On message.completed, deliver only non-empty visible text. Claim outbound using ctx.session.id plus turnId/stepIndex/sequence before SendblueClient.sendMessage; persist provider handle on success and uncertain state on indeterminate outcome.
Do not send reasoning, tool payloads/intermediate tool-call messages, secrets, error details, or status callbacks.
mandatoryVerification: Production/test call graphs match the SPEC and apps/agent remains the owner; no new package or generic channel abstraction is introduced.
Flat meaningful Effect.gen/Effect.fn channel programs with tagged errors handled in .pipe(...); the Eve file is a thin runPromise/ManagedRuntime adapter.
Canonical schemas and service contracts; no unsafe casts, DTO mirrors, manual object guards/readers/mappers, JSON.stringify/JSON.parse, raw fetch, swallowed errors, or trivial wrappers/helpers.

Direct Request/Response tests cover all auth/status/classification cases, one accepted session, same-handle sequential and concurrent replay, line confusion/loop prevention, and storage fail-closed behavior.

Event tests cover stable outbound coordinates, one provider send, duplicate replay suppression, tool/reasoning exclusion, over-limit response handling, provider failures, and uncertain no-auto-resend.
Eve build discovers the sendblue channel and does not expose secrets in info/metadata.
Agent checks and bun run verification pass.
completionCriteria: The channel handles a valid signed fixture end to end through a fake Eve send and memory Sendblue client.
Every invalid/ignored/replayed event has explicit HTTP and side-effect behavior.
All three audit passes are recorded.

evidence: Ownership/call graph: apps/agent owns the custom Eve channel, app-owned Sendblue service, and live Layer; the Eve filesystem entrypoint contains only ManagedRuntime composition, HTTP status mapping, waitUntil dispatch, and completed-message event projection, while provider/auth/identity/replay policy remains in agent/lib/sendblue.

Implementation quality: named flat Effect programs authenticate before one Schema JSON decode, classify with Match, derive canonical auth/state only after identity resolution, claim before dispatch/send, and apply owner-fenced complete/retryable/uncertain transitions; review corrected duplicate service injection, direction handling, pre-provider length validation, and completion-failure quarantine without unsafe casts, manual JSON, raw fetch, or helper sprawl.

Verification coverage: fifteen focused channel tests prove direct route 401/400/200/202/503 behavior, accepted waitUntil dispatch, sequential/concurrent replay, line/loop/identity guards, Eve rejection release, stable outbound coordinates, one concurrent provider send, visible-text filtering, over-limit and known-failure retryability, uncertain no-resend, and completion quarantine; agent typecheck/test/build, Knip, all workspace verification, diff checks, and Eve discovery with zero diagnostics pass.

tasks.title: Provision and prove the Sendblue channel on Vercel Preview
tasks.description: Configure isolated Preview credentials/storage/identity and a protected Sendblue receive webhook, then prove invalid, valid, concurrent replay, Eve routing, and one real outbound reply with sanitized evidence.
implementationNotes: Use only Cooper's personal Vercel and Sendblue resources. Scope API/webhook/routing/storage variables to Preview and keep them encrypted.
Redeploy from a clean committed source using only Vercel-managed variables; declare any Eve build-time discovery inputs in turbo.json if required.

Keep Deployment Protection active. If Sendblue cannot set its bypass header, use a dedicated x-vercel-protection-bypass query credential in the webhook URL; the route must independently require sb-signing-secret.
Configure a receive webhook object with a Preview secret through a documented operator command/API step. Do not print or commit the full protected URL.
Run direct missing/wrong-secret, malformed, unknown, ignored, storage-failure fixture, valid signed, and concurrent duplicate probes against the Preview route.

Send one minimal non-sensitive real iMessage from an allowed test sender, observe one Eve session and one outbound Sendblue reply, then replay the inbound handle and outbound Eve event proof with no duplicate.
Correlate only digests/suffixes for provider handles/conversation/session/event ids plus status/timing metadata. Inspect Vercel logs, Sendblue readback, and replay records for leaks.
Rotate/revoke the Vercel automation bypass if exposed and redeploy as Vercel requires. Production remains untouched.
mandatoryVerification: No Production env, webhook, storage namespace, line, alias, or deployment changes occur.
Preview is built from clean source using encrypted variables only; no inline secret/build-env flags or protected URL output.
Flat Effect proof/provision programs, tagged pipe error handling, canonical schemas, no unsafe casts/DTO mirrors/manual readers/raw JSON/helper sprawl.
The full SPEC proof matrix passes with direct HTTP statuses and side-effect counts.
One real inbound message produces exactly one accepted Eve dispatch and exactly one outbound provider handle; duplicate replay produces neither.
No credential, message text, full phone number, full webhook URL, raw provider body, or ciphertext appears in source, output, logs, snapshots, or proof artifacts.
Agent checks and bun run verification pass.
completionCriteria: The personal Vercel Preview has a live, authenticated, replay-protected Sendblue direct-message channel.
Sanitized preview evidence and rollback/rotation inputs are recorded in the active plan and ledger.
All three audit passes are recorded.

evidence: Ownership/call graph: personal Vercel owns encrypted Preview inputs, Deployment Protection, and the Preview-only Marketplace Upstash connection; Sendblue owns one receive webhook; the thin Eve route delegates authentication, identity, routing, replay, Codex dispatch, and provider delivery to the accepted app-owned Effect services. Production remained untouched.

Implementation quality: the immutable deployment uses the full public Eve route, app-owned replay Config with preferred-name precedence and provider KV fallback, independent platform and webhook credentials, and fail-closed durable claims. Review corrected the root route, missing build-time provider inputs, agent build dependency/test coverage, exposed approval credentials, and stale operator metadata without adding a provisioning helper layer, unsafe cast, manual runtime JSON, or helper sprawl.

Verification coverage: clean Preview deployment dpl_C2Xg1F8H8KFiARopc59WeDwKV7tQ from exact pushed commit fdb71a87 passed the post-rotation 401/400/200/202 route matrix and an isolated 503 storage fixture; exactly one receive webhook is registered; one provider-originated inbound produced one delivered outbound; sequential and concurrent synthetic replays plus replay of the real provider handle returned 200 with no message-count increase; Vercel error/fatal logs were empty and all evidence remained sanitized.

tasks.title: Reconcile Sendblue docs and run the final audits
tasks.description: Update architecture, app, root, SPEC, and execution-plan documentation to the verified implementation, then complete the three-pass audits and final repository gate.
implementationNotes: Update apps/agent/README.md with config names, route/auth, supported event matrix, identity/replay behavior, local tests, preview proof, credential rotation, failure handling, and rollback.
Update docs/architecture/eve-agent.md with production/test/CLI call graphs and docs/architecture/repo-structure.md only if the app-owned integration layout is now a durable convention.
Update README.md and ARCHITECTURE.md from future to Preview-verified only after live proof; do not imply Production promotion.
State explicitly that Sendblue supplies a shared signing-secret header rather than a body HMAC and that Vercel bypass does not replace route authentication.
Reconcile this SPEC/task ledger and active execution plan with actual files, operations, Layers, env names, proof ids, limitations, and follow-up work.
Run helper-sprawl, unsafe-cast, manual-reader/mapper, raw JSON, process.env, raw fetch, Map/Set replay, lint-suppression, PII/secret marker, stale-status, and untracked-runtime-artifact scans.
mandatoryVerification: Docs match the actual app-owned production/test/CLI call graphs and do not describe unsupported group/media/HITL/Production behavior as live.
Each implementation task has three or more recorded ownership, quality/helper-admission, and verification/evidence audit passes.
All source uses flat meaningful Effect flows, tagged pipe error handling, canonical schemas, and no unsafe casts/DTO mirrors/manual readers/mappers/raw JSON/trivial wrappers.
Agent targeted checks, bun run check, bun run knip, bun run verification, git diff --check, and task-ledger consistency checks pass.
No credentials, message content, full phone numbers, protected webhook URLs, raw provider bodies, or replay payloads are committed.
completionCriteria: Current documentation explains exactly how to configure, test, observe, rotate, and disable the verified Preview channel.
All mandatory audits and verification gates are green with sanitized evidence.
Production Sendblue enablement remains gated by the Vercel Production Promotion SPEC.

evidence: Ownership/call graph: root, app, architecture, repo-structure, SPEC, and plan docs now place the thin Eve adapter and provider-specific Effect services in apps/agent, describe the live Preview and deterministic test graphs, remove stale future-Sendblue claims, and keep Production promotion separately gated. Parent review returned and corrected stale repo tree, future-boundary, roadmap, and retry-state language.

Implementation quality: documented rules match the accepted canonical Schema-derived contracts, Redacted Config, Context services, explicit Layers, app-owned ManagedRuntime, named flat linear Effects, tagged pipe error handling, claim-before-side-effect ordering, and owner-fenced replay transitions. Runtime scans found no unsafe casts, manual JSON, direct process.env/raw fetch, JavaScript replay Map/Set, lint suppressions, DTO mirrors, or helper sprawl; frontend composition was correctly not applicable. Parent review also corrected rendered metadata and retry prose.

Verification coverage: agent typecheck and all 38 tests, root Ultracite, Knip, all workspace typechecks/tests, task-ledger consistency, and git diff checks pass. Leak/stale scans found no exact 32-hex credential, protected webhook URL, untracked runtime proof artifact, or stale Sendblue status; the only tracked env-like file is the empty .env.example and all E.164 values are synthetic test fixtures.

Final consistency: a post-audit read corrected historical provider-discovery and build entries that still used present or future tense and replaced the final ambiguous bounded-release phrase with the implemented owner-fenced release; formatting and diff checks remained green.

tasks.title: Enforce one active Production receive webhook
tasks.description: Correct the account-level Sendblue fan-out configuration by removing Preview ingress, retaining the stable Production webhook, and proving one handset message has exactly one Production execution path.
implementationNotes: Treat the existing two-webhook rollout as historical evidence, not an accepted invariant. Sendblue fans each account event out to every registered receive URL; environment-specific secrets and replay namespaces do not select one target or deduplicate across targets.

Before mutation, use the trusted Executor Personal Sendblue connection to read the current webhook inventory. Fail closed unless there are exactly two receive entries whose parsed hosts are the known immutable Preview host and stable Production host. Emit only sanitized counts, host classifications, and statuses.

Inside one trusted provider execution, select the exact receive entry by parsed Preview host, call deleteWebhooks with that exact value and type receive, then read back the provider inventory. Never return, print, persist, or commit the protected URL, query credential, webhook secret, provider credential, message content, number, or handle.

After provider readback proves only the stable Production receive webhook remains, revoke only the dedicated Preview Sendblue Vercel automation bypass. Do not change the Production bypass, route secret, encrypted variables, replay namespace, alias, deployment, model provider, Codex profile, or Executor policy.

Fail closed on any topology mismatch. If the Production entry is absent, restore only that known entry from the operator-owned secret record. Do not re-add Preview while Production remains registered. A failed Production proof keeps Preview disabled; emergency Preview cutover must first disable Production ingress and be explicitly time-bounded.

The expected tracked diff is documentation and ledger evidence only. No runtime TypeScript, TSX, package metadata, lint configuration, environment contract, provider-admin abstraction, or shared package is expected. If live proof identifies a code defect, stop the operator mutation slice and amend the SPEC before implementation.

Any later admitted CLI/proof command stays at the apps/agent executable edge and reuses the existing Sendblue owner. It uses canonical Effect Schema evidence, schema-derived types, Config/Redacted credentials, tagged errors, explicit live/test Layers, one named flat Effect.fn/Effect.gen success path, expected error handling in the outer pipe, and Effect.runPromise only at the executable edge.

Helper admission is per symbol and file. Inline one-off target selection, host classification, evidence projection, Effect.map aliases, predicates, and response constructors. Do not add WebhookRoutingService, SendblueAdminService, DTO mappers, static namespaces, pass-through Layers, generic proof frameworks, utils/helpers/common/shared modules, or speculative cross-channel abstractions.

Treat the compiled Sendblue implementation as the code-shape authority: SendblueChannelRuntimeLive composes SendblueChannelLive with the merged verifier, identity, router, replay, client, Config, FetchHttpClient, claim-id, and constant-time verification boundaries. Documentation must use the real route, service methods, canonical Schemas/errors, and Layer names rather than preserving aspirational examples.

Verify the Production Executor MCP endpoint is disjoint from Preview and exposes only skills/execute/resume. The historical four-GitHub execute discovery was caused by Production's final catch-all block, not a Preview binding. Preview remains GitHub-only with its block; Production executor.* and its final block are removed, so the selected non-Executor catalog is visible. Executor toolkit/policy/key/credential/OAuth/connection administration remains hard-denied; other selected provider operations use tool defaults plus reviewed overrides under an accepted temporary model-mode risk.

This task has no visible UI. Do not add a React route, dashboard, webhook health panel, replay viewer, credential form, component, hook, or browser bundle. Historical completion evidence used the former leaf-owned reads/mutations/commands wording; it is retained as evidence, not current policy. Any future UI requires a separate SPEC enforcing primitive -> composite -> layout -> route, route/feature-owned data loading, Effect execution, service/RPC calls, auth, mutations, commands, and shared workflows, presentation-leaf rendering/accessibility/local UI and loading/empty/error/retry/skeleton/fallback display from narrow values/callbacks, server-only Effect services and secrets, Schema-owned RPC contracts, and desktop/mobile Browser evidence.

Reconcile the Sendblue SPEC, production-promotion SPEC, active plans, apps/agent README, Eve architecture guide, and any root current-state statements. Preserve dated two-webhook evidence as historical and mark its independent-routing conclusion superseded.
mandatoryVerification: Pre- and post-mutation provider inventories are bounded and sanitized: exactly two known receive hosts before, exactly one stable Production receive host after, with all protected values omitted.
Production route probes preserve the accepted missing-secret 401, invalid-secret 401, and authenticated-malformed 400 behavior after the provider mutation.

One new minimal handset message produces exactly one Production webhook 2xx, one accepted Production Eve dispatch/turn, one correlated Production proxy completion sequence, and one Sendblue outbound terminal result in the bounded proof window. Multiple proxy completions are valid only when correlated to the single turn's model/tool continuation sequence.

The same bounded proof window contains zero Preview webhook requests, zero Preview Agent Run/session updates, zero Preview model or Executor execution, and no second Production dispatch, unrelated model cycle, duplicate tool execution, or duplicate outbound.

Production Executor direct MCP proof confirms the endpoint differs from Preview, the exact skills/execute/resume surface, completed removal of Production executor.* and its final catch-all block, selected non-Executor catalog visibility, and the Executor administration boundary without exposing endpoint, API key, user input, or tool output content.

If TypeScript changes, every primary operation is a flat meaningful Effect.gen/Effect.fn program; expected tagged errors are handled in .pipe(...) with catchTag, catchTags, or mapError; all boundary contracts are canonical Effect Schema-derived; and Effect language-service diagnostics have no unresolved actionable hints.

If TypeScript changes, live/test Layers are explicit at the composition root; domain operations contain no runPromise/runSync, Layer construction, process.env access, floating promises, nested Effect.gen without a resource/reuse reason, or provider/framework exceptions escaping their owning boundary.

No unsafe casts, any, non-null decoding bypass, DTO mirrors, manual object readers/mappers, JSON.stringify/JSON.parse, raw fetch, direct process.env, instanceof or raw _tag branching, generic helper modules, pass-through services, broad lint suppressions, or one-off provider wrappers are added.

git diff --name-only confirms no TypeScript, TSX, package metadata, lint config, ignore config, or frontend file changed; otherwise the amended SPEC, focused tests, Effect language-service diagnostics, and applicable Browser proof are present before acceptance.

A source-to-SPEC parity review confirms current names and contracts: SendblueChannelRuntimeLive, SendblueConfigService, SendblueReplayClaimIdGenerator, SendblueSessionRouter.route, claimInbound(message_handle), SendblueInboundDecision, SendblueClientError, empty HTTP responses, and the route-level catchTags status mapping. No stale directConversation, invented rejected-response DTO, raw cause retention, or imaginary Layer remains in current guidance.

No React/component/route/hook file changes. Historical completion evidence used the former leaf-owned state/command wording; it is retained as evidence, not current policy. If frontend scope is separately approved, verify canonical typography and design-system controls, primitive -> composite -> layout -> route composition, route/feature-owned data and workflow execution, presentation-leaf rendering/accessibility/local UI and state display, no prop drilling/generic workflow hooks, no Effect runtime in render/browser bundles, and desktop/mobile loading/empty/error/long-content screenshots.

Run focused agent typecheck/test/build, bun run check, bun run knip, bun run verification, jq empty on both relevant ledgers, git diff --check, stale dual-routing scans, leak scans, and clean tracked-state verification.
completionCriteria: The shared Sendblue account/line has exactly one active receive webhook and it targets the stable Production route.
The Preview Sendblue receive webhook and its dedicated automation bypass are inactive while historical Preview configuration/evidence remains clearly non-live.
Rollback and incident handling preserve mutual exclusion: Production and Preview ingress are never active together on the shared account/line.
A bounded live handset proof demonstrates one and only one Production ingress -> Eve -> Codex proxy -> Executor-policy-aware agent -> Sendblue outbound path, with zero Preview side effects.
Current docs, SPECs, task ledger, and execution plans state the single-active-webhook invariant and no current text claims dual webhooks are independently routed.
Three parent audit passes are recorded for ownership/call graph, Effect implementation quality/helper admission, and verification/evidence coverage; add passes until every finding is resolved.

evidence: 2026-07-16 operational evidence: trusted Executor Personal pre-inventory found exactly two known receive hosts; the exact Preview receive entry was deleted and immediate readback found exactly one stable Production host. The dedicated Preview Sendblue Vercel automation bypass was then revoked; the distinct Production bypass remained. Direct missing-secret and invalid-secret Production probes both returned 401. A bounded post-change window had no Preview runtime request or Agent Run. Historical Production Executor discovery returned skills/execute/resume and four GitHub operations; subsequent authoritative readback diagnosed the Production final catch-all policy block, not a Preview endpoint binding, as the reason its configured catalog was hidden. No protected URL, credential, content, number, handle, provider body, or tool output was retained.

2026-07-16 parent audit pass 1, ownership/call graph: parent review confirmed the correction remained provider state plus documentation only. Sendblue owns account delivery, Vercel owns the targeted bypass, apps/agent remains the channel/runtime owner, and no app/package/config/alias/model/Codex-profile/Executor-policy surface changed. The parent returned four stale current-state claims to the worker; the same worker corrected them, and a new stale scan leaves dual-webhook statements only inside explicitly dated historical evidence.

2026-07-16 parent audit pass 2, implementation quality/helper admission: parent git diff and source-parity review confirmed no TypeScript, TSX, package, lint, ignore, frontend, provider-admin abstraction, or helper change. Current documentation retains the compiled SendblueChannelRuntimeLive, SendblueReplayClaimIdGenerator, SendblueSessionRouter.route, message-handle claim, canonical decision/client-error, empty-response, and catchTags boundaries. A parent diff leak scan found no protected URL, query credential, authorization value, secret assignment, or live Executor endpoint.

2026-07-16 bounded post-request readback: from 11:10Z through 11:21Z, Sendblue returned zero inbound and zero outbound records; Production and Preview runtime-log queries returned zero records; Production and Preview Agent Run queries returned zero runs; and the Production proxy runtime-log query returned zero records. No handset inbound exists in this bounded interval, so no one-path/zero-Preview/one-proxy/one-outbound or duplicate-absence proof can be claimed.

2026-07-16 accepted authenticated-malformed proof: one ephemeral interactive shell loaded the labeled Production Webhook Secret and the populated current Vercel Production Bypass field from authenticated 1Password into shell memory. Curl received the protected URL and sb-signing-secret only through stdin config, discarded response body and headers, returned HTTP 400 with curl exit 0, then both variables were unset and the shell exited. No protected value or full URL was emitted or persisted.

2026-07-16 final bounded handset readback: from 11:21:00Z through 11:39:53Z, Sendblue returned zero inbound and zero outbound records. Production and Preview Agent Run queries each returned zero runs. Production agent runtime evidence contained only the accepted status-400 authenticated-malformed probe; Preview agent and Production proxy runtime queries had zero activity. No handset-originated path, outbound, or duplicate-absence evidence exists in this window.

2026-07-16 Executor policy-scope provider evidence: guarded readback found Production 17 connections with exactly one executor.* and one final wildcard block, then removed only that connection and block. Production now has 16 selected non-Executor namespaces, four GitHub overrides, zero Executor administration operations, and zero wildcard blocks; Preview remains one GitHub connection, five policies, and one wildcard block. No downstream provider operation, endpoint, key, Vercel variable, Sendblue state, deployment, or app code changed.

2026-07-16 accepted handset proof: the bounded 12:03:30Z through 12:09:30Z window contained exactly one Sendblue RECEIVED iMessage inbound, one Production Sendblue webhook request, one accepted Production turn, two successful Production proxy completions in that single turn's expected model/tool continuation sequence, and exactly one DELIVERED iMessage outbound. Preview runtime readback returned zero requests; agent and proxy error/fatal queries returned zero records. The user confirmed the deployed reply found the broader Production catalog. No content, number, handle, endpoint, credential, provider body, prompt, tool output, or model output was retained.
